name: elementor-mcp/sideload-image
label: Sideload Image
description: Downloads an external image URL into the WordPress Media Library and returns the local attachment ID and URL. Use this after search-images to import a chosen image.

url.description: The external image URL to download.

title.description: Attachment title. Falls back to the filename.

attribution.description: Attribution text for Creative Commons images. Stored as the attachment caption/excerpt.

name: elementor-mcp/add-stock-image
label: Add Stock Image
description: Searches Openverse for an image, downloads it to the Media Library, and adds it as an image widget to the page — all in one step. Defaults to landscape (wide) images for consistent layouts. Combines search-images + sideload-image + add-image.

query.description: Image search keywords (e.g. "hero banner technology", "team photo office").

index.description: Which search result to use (0 = best match). Default: 0.

caption.description: Caption override. Defaults to Openverse attribution.

aspect_ratio.description: Image aspect ratio filter. Default: wide (landscape). Use "wide" for hero banners and card images, "tall" for sidebar/portrait, "square" for thumbnails, "any" for no filter.

alt_text.description: Alt text override. Defaults to the image title.

name: elementor-mcp/upload-svg-icon
label: Upload SVG Icon
description: Uploads an SVG icon to the WordPress Media Library and returns an Elementor icon object ready to use with any widget that accepts icons (icon, icon-box, button, etc.). Accepts either an external SVG URL or raw SVG markup. The returned icon object has the format: { "value": { "id": 123, "url": "..." }, "library": "svg" }. Use this value for the selected_icon setting in icon/icon-box widgets, or the selected_icon setting in button widgets.

svg_url.description: External URL to an SVG file to download and import. Use this OR svg_content, not both.

svg_content.description: Raw SVG markup string (e.g. "<svg viewBox='0 0 24 24'><path d='M12 ...'/></svg>"). Use this OR svg_url, not both.

title.description: Title for the SVG in the Media Library. Falls back to filename or "Custom SVG Icon".

icon_object.description: Ready-to-use Elementor icon object. Pass this directly as the selected_icon setting.

name: elementor-mcp/add-custom-js
label: Add Custom JavaScript
description: Adds a custom JavaScript snippet to a page by inserting an HTML widget containing a <script> tag. Works with free Elementor (no Pro required). The JS code is automatically wrapped in <script> tags — do NOT include them yourself. Use wrap_dom_ready=true to wrap in a DOMContentLoaded listener. For site-wide JS, use add-code-snippet instead (requires Pro).

js.description: JavaScript code to inject. Do NOT include <script> tags — they are added automatically.

wrap_dom_ready.description: Wrap the code in a DOMContentLoaded listener. Default: false.

name: elementor-mcp/add-custom-css
label: Add Custom CSS
description: Adds custom CSS to a specific element or to the entire page. Requires Elementor Pro. For element-level CSS, use the keyword "selector" as a placeholder for the element's CSS wrapper (e.g. "selector .heading { color: red; }" or "selector:hover { transform: scale(1.05); }"). For page-level CSS, omit element_id. Appends to existing CSS by default; set replace=true to overwrite.

element_id.description: Optional element ID to apply CSS to. If omitted, CSS is applied at the page level.

css.description: CSS rules to add. Use "selector" as the element wrapper placeholder for element-level CSS.

replace.description: If true, replaces existing custom CSS instead of appending. Default: false.

name: elementor-mcp/add-code-snippet
label: Add Code Snippet
description: Creates a site-wide Custom Code snippet using Elementor Pro. Injects CSS or JavaScript into the <head>, after <body> open, or before </body> close on ALL pages. Use this for analytics scripts, site-wide CSS overrides, meta tags, or tracking pixels. Requires Elementor Pro and manage_options capability.

title.description: Descriptive title for the snippet (e.g. "Google Analytics", "Global CSS overrides").

code.description: The full code to inject. Include <script>, <style>, or <meta> tags as needed.

location.description: Where to inject: "head" = <head> tag, "body_start" = after <body>, "body_end" = before </body>. Default: head.

priority.description: Load order priority (1-10, lower = earlier). Default: 1.

status.description: Post status. "publish" = active immediately. "draft" = saved but not active. Default: publish.

ensure_jquery.description: If true, ensures jQuery is loaded before this snippet runs. Default: false.

name: elementor-mcp/list-code-snippets
label: List Code Snippets
description: Lists all existing Elementor Pro Custom Code snippets with their titles, locations, priorities, and statuses. Requires Elementor Pro.

status.description: Filter by post status. Default: any.
